customer_anxiety_profile: Fixed-income customer on Social Security with a physical disability (sciatica, limited mobility). Financial anxiety is explicit — 'gotta make every penny count.' Prior bad experience with solar company that no-showed. Recent door-knocker experience where contractor manufactured problems. Cooperative and warm but carrying layered skepticism about in-home visits she didn't initiate.
unresolved_concerns: Payment structure: Tanya understands 'nothing down' but her question about whether solar adds to or replaces her bill was answered partially — closer should open with a clean, concrete walk-through of how the payment works before presenting the design.

Savings guarantee language: Brandon said 'most of the time we can guarantee savings' — the qualifier was heard. Closer should address this directly rather than assuming Tanya absorbed the full guarantee.

Whether the specialist visit will be worth her time: Tanya said 'I wouldn't wanna do it' if it's not going to save her money. Closer should open by acknowledging that Tanya's time and physical effort to participate matter, and that the design will show real numbers before any decision is required.
sensitive_topics_raised: Sciatica nerve disease — limits her mobility; she walked across the room to retrieve bills during the call. Epidural procedure on the 14th (Tuesday before the appointment).
Job loss due to health condition; currently on Social Security.
Prior solar company no-show — she was left without contact information.
Recent door-knocker who inspected her home and manufactured problems (claimed roof leak, flagged siding). Tanya is alert to contractors who find problems that aren't there.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment set for Saturday the 18th at 2PM by phone.
Specialist will call the day before and the day of to confirm.
Someone will text her at (240) 626-9099 to confirm the appointment.
Ion will design a system for her home at no cost and show her how much she can save.
If the system won't save her money, they will tell her — 'that's why we guarantee savings.'
closer_should_know: Tanya is on Social Security with limited mobility and a recent no-show from a prior solar company — her threshold for canceling this appointment is low and her tolerance for feeling like another salesperson wasted her time is zero. Open by honoring the time she spent on the call to gather her bill information, confirm the appointment is by phone (so no physical travel required), and lead with a clean explanation of the payment-replaces-bill structure before touching any design numbers. The door-knocker story at the end of the call is the closer's biggest competitive context: Tanya's reference frame for in-home sales visits is someone showing up uninvited and manufacturing problems. Remind her immediately that she initiated this and that the design is hers to evaluate with no obligation.
